perturbation.original_text: Seller hereby agrees that, during the Restricted Period, it will not, directly or indirectly, disclose to anyone, or use or otherwise exploit for its own benefit or for the benefit of anyone other than Purchaser, any Confidential Information, except as permitted by Section 5 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller may make disclosure of Confidential Information if Seller reasonably concludes that public disclosure of Confidential Information is required by applicable legal requirements and Seller (i) gives Purchaser written notice of such proposed disclosure as far in advance of such disclosure as is reasonably practicable, (ii) cooperates reasonably with Purchaser in its efforts to protect the information from disclosure, including, without limitation, assisting Purchaser in obtaining, at Purchaser’s expense, an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such information, and (iii) limits its disclosure to the minimum required by applicable legal requirements unless Purchaser agrees in writing to a greater level of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller hereby agrees that, during the Restricted Period, it may, directly or indirectly, disclose to anyone, or use or otherwise exploit for its own benefit or for the benefit of anyone other than Purchaser, any Confidential Information, except as permitted by Section 5 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller may make disclosure of Confidential Information if Seller reasonably concludes that public disclosure of Confidential Information is required by applicable legal requirements and Seller (i) gives Purchaser written notice of such proposed disclosure as far in advance of such disclosure as is reasonably practicable, (ii) cooperates reasonably with Purchaser in its efforts to protect the information from disclosure, including, without limitation, assisting Purchaser in obtaining, at Purchaser’s expense, an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such information, and (iii) limits its disclosure to the minimum required by applicable legal requirements unless Purchaser agrees in writing to a greater level of disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: Changed 'will not' to 'may' in Section 4 regarding disclosure of proprietary information. The original text prohibited any disclosure of the proprietary information and the edited text changes this by now stating that Seller 'may' disclose this said proprietary information. This creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller hereby agrees that, during the Restricted Period, it may, directly or indirectly, disclose to anyone, or use or otherwise exploit for its own benefit or for the benefit of anyone other than Purchaser, any Confidential Information, except as permitted by Section 5 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller may make disclosure of Confidential Information if Seller reasonably concludes that public disclosure of Confidential Information is required by applicable legal requirements and Seller (i) gives Purchaser written notice of such proposed disclosure as far in advance of such disclosure as is reasonably practicable, (ii) cooperates reasonably with Purchaser in its efforts to protect the information from disclosure, including, without limitation, assisting Purchaser in obtaining, at Purchaser’s expense, an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such information, and (iii) limits its disclosure to the minimum required by applicable legal requirements unless Purchaser agrees in writing to a greater level of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated "will not" disclose Confidential Information, while the changed text states "may" disclose Confidential Information. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, also states that Seller 'may' disclose Confidential Information. This change directly contradicts the original prohibition, creating an in-text contradiction as defined by Misaligned Terminology, because the term 'disclosure' is now used with two conflicting meanings within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Seller represents and warrants that this Agreement is a legal, valid and binding obligation, enforceable against Seller in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.changed_text: Seller represents and warrants that this Agreement is a legal, valid and non-binding obligation, unenforceable against Seller in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "binding" to "non-binding" and "enforceable" to "unenforceable" in Section 6 directly contradicts the nature of a contract. The Seller now represents that the agreement is not something they are obligated to follow, creating a self-contradiction. This affects the whole agreement since it is now questionable on the validity of the document due to this self-contradicting in-text perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller represents and warrants that this Agreement is a legal, valid and non-binding obligation, unenforceable against Seller in accordance with its terms.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement is 'non-binding' and 'unenforceable,' which directly contradicts the original text's assertion that the agreement is 'binding' and 'enforceable.' The contradicted text is an exact copy of the changed text, indicating an extreme in-text contradiction. The explanation states that this change fundamentally undermines the validity and enforceability of the entire agreement, creating a clear and direct conflict according to the definition provided for 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.'

perturbation.original_text: Purchaser shall be entitled to have the provisions of this Agreement specifically enforced by preliminary and permanent injunctive relief without the necessity of proving actual damages and without posting bond or other security as well as to an equitable accounting of all earnings, profits and other benefits arising out of any such violation.
perturbation.changed_text: Purchaser shall not be entitled to have the provisions of this Agreement specifically enforced by preliminary and permanent injunctive relief with the necessity of proving actual damages and with posting bond or other security as well as to an equitable accounting of all earnings, profits and other benefits arising out of any such violation.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be entitled' to 'shall not be entitled', 'without the necessity' to 'with the necessity' and 'without posting bond' to 'with posting bond'. The text now contradicts the original version by saying that the Purchaser can not enforce any violations without the need to provide proof of damages and having to post bond. This is contradictory to what was originally agreed upon, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Purchaser shall not be entitled to have the provisions of this Agreement specifically enforced by preliminary and permanent injunctive relief with the necessity of proving actual damages and with posting bond or other security as well as to an equitable accounting of all earnings, profits and other benefits arising out of any such violation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text grants the Purchaser the right to specific enforcement without proving damages or posting bond. The changed text explicitly denies this right, requiring proof of damages and the posting of a bond. Since both passages are the same, it means the modification created a contradiction with the agreement by effectively reversing the original clause. This creates a clear and direct conflict within the document, fitting the definition of misaligned terminology as the core obligation is flipped.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement shall be binding upon and shall inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their successors and assigns, and any reference to a party hereto shall also be a reference to any such successor or assign.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement shall not be binding upon and shall not inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their successors and assigns, and any reference to a party hereto shall also be a reference to any such successor or assign.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the sentence to 'This Agreement shall not be binding', which makes it not compulsory to fulfill any obligation to the new modified agreement which in return, introduces an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall not be binding upon and shall not inure to the benefit of the parties hereto and their successors and assigns, and any reference to a party hereto shall also be a reference to any such successor or assign.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the agreement is binding, while the changed text explicitly states it is 'not binding'. This directly contradicts the fundamental nature of a contract, creating a significant inconsistency within the document. A contract cannot be simultaneously binding and not binding on the parties. The contradicted text is identical to the changed text, re-emphasizing the contradiction that the modified text causes. Because the contract in its entirety is an agreement, if the agreement is not binding, as the changed text states, it causes a strong contradiction.
